text.title: Clean Architecture Decision Tree
text.summary: Routing tree from observable signal to a rule id in `01-core-rules.xml`. Every leaf resolves to either a rule id or `skip-this-methodology`.

decision-tree.root-question: Which observable signal best describes the current state of the artefact under review?

branch.question: Is the codebase a CRUD prototype with no domain logic?

conclusion.verdict: use Transaction Script; layered architecture has no payoff yet

branch.question: Does a domain entity import the ORM or web framework?

conclusion.verdict: invert: declare an interface in the entity layer; let the framework implement

branch.question: Are entity tests slower than 100ms aggregate?

conclusion.verdict: remove IO from the test path; use in-memory state

branch.question: Does a use case modify >1 aggregate in one transaction?

conclusion.verdict: split into two saves; model the partial-failure path explicitly

branch.question: Are domain events published before persist commits?

conclusion.verdict: reconsider boundary or use a mapping library
signal does not match; route to a different methodology or proceed without this one

section.title: Usage
section.p: Apply the tree top-to-bottom against the artefact (PR diff, config, design doc). The first matching branch is decisive; later branches are not evaluated. If multiple branches match, fix in the order listed — they typically depend on earlier fixes.
